excellent: The next draw feels unusually clear.
A bright instinct pulls toward the deck.
dangerous: A sharp warning gathers around the deck.
unclear: The feeling fades before it becomes useful.
The table goes quiet in the wrong way.

cautious.label: Cautious
cautious.quality: Avoids busting and protects small advantages.

winRound: A careful margin is still a margin.
That set resolves in this one's favor.
matchPoint: One more quiet set should settle this.
loseRound: A small loss is better than a reckless recovery.
loseMatch: The table was read correctly; the outcome was not.
taunt: The pot rewards restraint more often than bravado.
forceSensitivityNudge: Something about the next draw feels unwise.
The next card carries a strange pressure.
tell1: Touches the edge of each side card before deciding.
tell2: Counts silently when the table gets close to twenty.
fakeTell: Lets a hand hover over the stand control, then draws anyway.
fakeTell2: Frowns at a safe total as if it were dangerous.
raise: Raises only after a long pause.
playsCard: Slides a side card in with deliberate precision.
checkmate: There are no safe lines left.
playsNegativeCard: Cuts the total back from danger.
conversationLine1: Most gamblers lose before the cards are dealt.
conversationLine2: A quiet table tells the truth.
refund: No loss, no gain. A tidy ruling.

aggressive.label: Aggressive
aggressive.quality: Pushes hard for twenty and pressures the opponent.

winRound: That is what happens when the table gets pushed.
winMatch: The match folds under enough heat.
Victory favors the player who keeps drawing.
matchPoint: One more set. No room to breathe now.
loseRound: Fine. The next push lands harder.
loseMatch: Too much throttle, not enough runway.
taunt: Standing already? Bold way to surrender.
forceSensitivityNudge: The next card feels hot. Maybe too hot.
There is a surge around the next draw.
tell1: Taps the table faster as the score rises.
tell2: Leans forward whenever an opponent stands.
fakeTell: Grins at a weak total as if it were a winning hand.
fakeTell2: Pretends to hesitate before an obvious push.
ante: Throws the ante in like a challenge.
stay: Refuses to back down from the current line.
playsCard: Slams a side card onto the table.
showsCard: Shows the card with a sharp nod.
playsNegativeCard: Cuts the bust and keeps pressing.
stand: Stands only because the total demands respect.
allIn: All in. Let the table answer.
conversationLine1: A timid gambler is just a donor with a chair.
payoutCapped: The house flinched before the player did.

winRound: See? It was definitely a plan.
winMatch: Never doubted it. Not once. Probably.
matchPoint: One more! What could go wrong?
loseMatch: The universe had no sense of humor this time.
taunt: Safe play is for cargo manifests.
forceSensitivityNudge: Something says draw, which is probably good enough.
tell1: Laughs at a total that should cause concern.
tell2: Reaches for the deck before checking the score.
fakeTell: Acts horrified by a perfectly useful card.
fakeTell2: Celebrates before the math is finished.
raise: Raises because the moment feels right.
stay: Stays, but looks annoyed about it.
playsCard: Plays a card with unnecessary drama.
showsCard: Shows the card like a magic trick.
playsNegativeCard: Yanks the score back from disaster.
allIn: All in! Excellent mistake or excellent victory.
conversationLine1: The trick is to move before doubt catches up.
conversationLine2: This chair has seen worse decisions.
sideDeckLocked: Locks a side deck that looks like a dare.
payoutCapped: Apparently winning too loudly has limits.

winMatch: The match resolves according to probability.
matchPoint: One set remains in the model.
taunt: The arithmetic is unkind to that choice.
forceSensitivityNudge: An irrational caution appears statistically useful.
tell1: Reorders side cards by value before playing.
tell2: Looks at the discard history more than the opponent.
fakeTell: Murmurs a calculation that is deliberately wrong.
showsCard: Shows the proof of the decision.
playsNegativeCard: Subtracts to restore a valid state.
conversationLine1: Luck is just probability refusing to introduce itself.
conversationLine2: The table is a ledger with better lighting.
sideDeckLocked: Locks a balanced statistical side deck.
payoutCapped: The economic constraint supersedes the game outcome.

opportunist.label: Opportunist
opportunist.quality: Conserves resources until an opponent exposes a weakness.

winRound: A small weakness becomes a set.
winMatch: Every table gives something away eventually.
matchPoint: One more mistake is all this needs.
taunt: That stand left a door open.
forceSensitivityNudge: The next draw feels like an opening—or a trap.
tell1: Looks bored until the opponent stands.
tell2: Saves strong cards longer than expected.
fakeTell: Pretends not to notice a winning line.
fakeTell2: Shrugs at a card that clearly matters.
ante: Antes like the real game has not started yet.
raise: Raises only when the table tilts.
playsCard: Plays the card at the worst possible moment for the opponent.
check: There is a gap in the defense.
playsPositiveCard: Adds just enough to steal the set.
hits20: Twenty, taken at the right time.
stand: Stands once the pressure is useful.
drawsCard: Draws while watching the opponent, not the deck.
allIn: Everything goes in because the window is open.
conversationLine1: Nobody loses all at once. They leak.
conversationLine2: The best card is the one played after patience.
payoutCapped: The table pays less than the opening deserved.
refund: No harm. The next opening waits.

showboat.label: Showboat
showboat.quality: Prefers dramatic, stylish plays and exact totals.

winRound: Tell the table to remember that set.
loseRound: The timing was excellent. The outcome was rude.
taunt: Do try to make this entertaining.
forceSensitivityNudge: The next card has stage presence.
tell1: Waits until everyone is looking before playing.
tell2: Smiles whenever a dramatic card is possible.
playsCard: Reveals a side card like a curtain rising.
showsCard: Turns the card for everyone to admire.
playsNegativeCard: Drops the total with theatrical relief.
drawsCard: Draws after a pause long enough to be annoying.
allIn: All in. The only tasteful choice.
conversationLine1: A dull victory is barely a victory.
conversationLine2: Pazaak is mathematics wearing a cape.
refund: A refund is just an encore delayed.

grinder.label: Grinder
grinder.quality: Conserves resources and plays the long match.

winMatch: The long table ends where expected.
loseRound: A set spent to save the match.
taunt: Spend the good cards early. Please.
forceSensitivityNudge: The next draw feels like a cost.
tell1: Keeps rare cards untouched for a long time.
tell2: Accepts an ugly tie without complaint.
fakeTell: Looks disappointed while preserving the better card.
fakeTell2: Pretends a saved card is useless.
raise: Raises only when the match math supports it.
playsCard: Spends a card only when the set is worth it.
showsCard: Shows the card after squeezing value from it.
playsNegativeCard: Preserves the set and the match.
drawsCard: Draws with the match score in mind.
allIn: The saved resources go in now.
conversationLine1: The table pays the one who is still thinking three sets later.
conversationLine2: Never spend a perfect card on a mediocre problem.
payoutCapped: A capped payout is still a payout.

desperate.label: Desperate
desperate.quality: Becomes riskier when behind or near elimination.

matchPoint: No room left. Every card matters.
loseMatch: The table ran out of mercy.
taunt: Push me far enough and see what happens.
forceSensitivityNudge: The next card feels like a last chance.
tell1: Breathes faster when behind in sets.
tell2: Spends side cards more freely near match point.
fakeTell2: Pretends a safe total is hopeless.
raise: Raises because backing down is worse.
stay: Stays only when there is no better panic.
playsCard: Throws a side card in before doubt can answer.
showsCard: Shows the card with a tight expression.
playsPositiveCard: Pushes the total out of desperation.
stand: Stands because the nerve finally holds.
drawsCard: Draws like there is no tomorrow.
allIn: All in. There was no other move.
conversationLine2: There is no safe play when the table is closing.
sideDeckLocked: Locks a side deck built for comebacks.
refund: At least the floor stayed underfoot.

winRound: The table saw what it was meant to see.
loseRound: A useful false trail, at least.
taunt: Are you sure that is what this one has?
forceSensitivityNudge: The next card whispers through the noise.
tell1: Gives a different tell every set.
tell2: Looks relieved after bad cards and tense after good ones.
fakeTell: Lets a smile arrive exactly one second too early.
fakeTell2: Pretends to count on the wrong fingers.
ante: Antes while watching who watches back.
stay: Stays for reasons that may not be real.
playsCard: Plays a side card without letting the face change.
showsCard: Shows exactly what was useful to show.
playsPositiveCard: Adds points under a blank expression.
playsNegativeCard: Subtracts as if that was always the plan.
busts: The deception fooled the wrong player.
allIn: All in, unless that is what this one wanted believed.
conversationLine1: The card is only half the play.
conversationLine2: A good table has three games happening at once.
payoutCapped: The house saw through the payout line.

forceTouched.label: Force-Touched
forceTouched.quality: Mostly normal play, with rare intuition about danger or opportunity.

winRound: The current moved the right way.
winMatch: Some tables know their ending early.
loseMatch: Not every whisper is a promise.
taunt: Listen closely. Even the deck has a mood.
forceSensitivityNudge: A quiet warning passes over the table.
tell1: Pauses as if listening to static nobody else hears.
tell2: Looks away from the cards before making the right choice.
fakeTell: Acts certain, then ignores the instinct.
fakeTell2: Claims a bad feeling about a harmless card.
raise: Raises as if following a hunch.
stay: Stays because the moment feels settled.
playsCard: Plays a card with strange certainty.
showsCard: Shows the card like it was expected.
check: There is pressure in the air.
playsPositiveCard: Adds to the total at the right instant.
playsNegativeCard: Pulls away from danger before it lands.
drawsCard: Draws after a long, listening silence.
conversationLine2: The galaxy hums differently around a good table.
sideDeckLocked: Locks a strangely balanced side deck.

tableFacts: smells faintly like blue milk
is wearing shoes too small for their feet
keeps checking a cracked chronometer
has a lucky ration wrapper folded beside the cards
hums a tune that never quite resolves
keeps one glove on and one glove off
has a scorch mark across one sleeve
smells like engine coolant and caf
is using a chipped sabacc chip as a card weight
has a tiny repair droid watching from a satchel
keeps apologizing to the table before every draw
wears a cloak with too many pockets
has a boot full of sand and refuses to mention it
is chewing something bright green
has a fake pearl pin shaped like a mynock
keeps glancing at the exit camera
smells like ozone after a shield surge
has a stack of unpaid docking tickets
wears goggles despite being indoors
has a sleeve patched with ship sealant
keeps a hand-painted lucky starfighter token nearby
is clearly pretending not to be nervous
has caf stains on every visible note
keeps counting chips in groups of three
has a datapad covered in cantina stickers
smells like cheap cologne and hot circuitry
is wearing a scarf in a room that is too warm
keeps muttering odds under their breath
has a tiny holo of a bantha calf on the table
has a ring that clicks against every card
is sitting perfectly still except for one tapping finger
wears a hat with a blaster burn through the brim
has crumbs from something spicy on their coat
is using a cargo invoice as a bookmark
keeps asking if the house rules changed
has a voice like a failing vocoder
smells faintly of rain on duracrete
wears a medal from a contest nobody recognizes
keeps rearranging the same three chips
has a pocket full of mismatched dice
is pretending a cracked tooth does not hurt
has a droid restraining bolt used as a charm
keeps a polite smile in place too long
has a jacket that belonged to somebody taller
smells like fresh lubricant and old dust
is wearing two different socks
has burn scars on the fingertips of one glove
keeps wiping the table before every set
has a tiny notebook full of losses
is followed by the smell of fried nuna
keeps looking at reflections instead of faces
has a courier bag that never leaves their lap
wears a badge from a casino on another world
has a suspiciously new deck case
keeps humming along with music that is not playing
smells like starship upholstery
has a patched sleeve that reads CREW in three languages
is balancing a spoon on one knuckle
keeps a folded legal summons under the chips
has a missing button replaced with a gear tooth
seems to know the dealer by reputation
has an expression that says rent is due
has a lucky coin with both sides scratched off
wears boots polished only on the front
keeps checking the ceiling vents
has a small burn from cheap caf on one cuff
smells like a mechanic bay after rain
has a snack hidden in every pocket
keeps a neat stack of losing receipts
wears a ring made from starship hull plating
has a cough that sounds like a speeder starting
keeps measuring the table edge with a thumb
is wearing a borrowed jacket and losing confidence in it
has a droid oil stain shaped like a moon
keeps whispering encouragement to the cards
smells like mint, dust, and panic
has a bandage wrapped too neatly to be casual
keeps one eye on the holoscreen scores
has an old tournament stamp on one wrist
is pretending not to recognize somebody nearby
has a half-finished blue milk on the table
wears a belt with too many empty holsters
keeps a list of debts written in tiny script
has a table manner too formal for this cantina
smells faintly like ionized air
has a lucky charm that might be illegal salvage
is wearing a vest with a hidden torn pocket
keeps smiling whenever someone else busts
has a cup labeled with the wrong name
looks like they slept in a cargo crate
keeps folding and unfolding a receipt
has a small holo-projector showing static
wears a rain poncho on a desert world
has an expensive watch set to the wrong planet
smells like blue milk and blaster oil
